calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Vacated

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. LISA VILLASENOR OF THE HASSELL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 334-4111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ZACHARY B. YOUNG OF ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (925) 930-6600 EXT. 502 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SUPERSHUTTLE OF SAN FRANCICO ET. AL. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF IVAN RODRIGUEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions - The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) The motion is denied as to form interrogatory numbers 7.1, 7.2, and 7.3. The form interrogatories call for the responding party to state if responding party is making any claim for property damage. 7.1(a) is a "yes or no" question, and the defendants responded "no." As a result, the subparts of 7.1, and the supplemental questions in 7.2 and 7.3 are not applicable. (2) The motion is granted as to form interrogatories 13.1 and 13.2. (a) Interrogatories 13.1 and 13.2 must be answered. Defendants response must include a Yes or No answer as to whether surveillance was conducted. If yes, then information pertaining to the target of the surveillance and a time frame of the month and calendar year that the surveillance was conducted must be provided. (b) As to surveillance conducted at the instigation of litigation defense counsel, a qualified work product privilege might apply under Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, but defendant has not met its burden to establish the privilege. The Court does not find that the disclosure of whether there were surveillance videos alone qualifies for an absolute work product privilege. (3) The motion with respect to Requests for Production numbers 10 and 11 is granted in part and denied in part as follows: (a) The defendants must produce any or all photographs or video footage obtained or produced by the parties themselves, their employees, agents or contractors. (b) The motion is denied without prejudice as to any photographs or video footage produced by or at the specific direction of defendant's litigation counsel. Should subsequent answers to form interrogatory numbers 13.1 and 13.2 disclose the existence of unprivileged surveillance photographs or videos, the motion to compel production could be renewed. If defense counsel decides to use any product of surveillance, including reports, photographs or video evidence at trial then defense counsel must disclose said surveillance evidence to be received by Plaintiff's counsel by close of business of September 29, 2014, via fax, e-mail or hard copy. (4) The request for sanctions is denied. The objections were substantially justified. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/mjm
